title: Null pointers should not be dereferenced
description: Null pointers should not be dereferencedAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugcwesymbolic-executionWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoAccessing anullvalue will always throw aNullReferenceExceptionmost likely causing an abrupt program termination.Such termination might expose sensitive information that a malicious third party could exploit to, for instance, bypass security measures.ExceptionsIn the following cases, the rule does not raise:Extensions MethodsCalls to extension methods can still operate onnullvalues.using System; using System.Text.RegularExpressions; public static class Program { public static string RemoveVowels(this string value) { if (value == null) { return null; } return Regex.Replace(value, "[aeoui]*","", RegexOptions.IgnoreCase); } public stat
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

logic.triggering_conditions: MethodDeclaration nodes that contain assignments to null followed by member access without null checks
logic.reasoning: Detects potential null pointer dereferences where variables are assigned null and then accessed without proper null checks. Uses regex to identify assignment to null followed by member access patterns across multiple lines while excluding safe navigation operators and null checks. Allows triggering in methods with logging or comments as these don't prevent NullReferenceExceptions.

should_not_trigger: [Test] public void Test() { text = null; Assert.ThrowsException(() => text.Length); }
